content_en:
The primary means of protection against magic, jinn possession, and the evil eye in Islam is the regular recitation and application of specific Quranic passages and prophetic supplications. Wahid Abdussalam Bali presents these protective prescriptions not as folklore or superstition but as part of the prophetic Sunnah — specific recommendations given by the Prophet, peace be upon him, for the protection of believers against spiritual harms.

Ayat al-Kursi (Al-Baqarah 2:255) holds the highest position among the protective verses of the Quran. The Prophet described it as the greatest verse in the Quran and gave it extraordinary protective significance. He stated: 'Whoever recites Ayat al-Kursi after every obligatory prayer, nothing will prevent him from entering Paradise except death.' He also taught that whoever recites it before sleeping will be protected by an angel of Allah throughout the night. The theological content of this verse — its affirmation of Allah's absolute sovereignty, His knowledge encompassing all things, His Kursi extending over the heavens and earth — makes it the most powerful expression of the foundations upon which Islamic protection rests.

The last two chapters of the Quran — Al-Falaq and An-Nas, collectively called al-Mu'awwidhatayn (the two chapters of seeking refuge) — were given by the Prophet specific applications as protective recitations. He taught that reciting them three times morning and evening provides protection throughout the day and night. Their content directly addresses the sources of harm that the believer seeks protection from: Al-Falaq seeks refuge from the evil in everything that was created, from darkness, from magic (specifically the 'evil of those who blow on knots'), and from the evil eye; An-Nas seeks refuge from the whispering of the devil, who whispers into the hearts of people. The Prophet reportedly recited these chapters over himself when ill and also recommended them to his companions.

Surat al-Baqarah as a whole, or its recitation in the home, provides powerful protection according to several authentic hadiths. The Prophet stated: 'Recite Surat al-Baqarah, for holding to it is a blessing and abandoning it is a regret, and magicians cannot overcome it.' He also said: 'Indeed, the devil flees from any house in which Surat al-Baqarah is recited.' Bali recommends regular recitation of al-Baqarah in the home — whether by listening to recorded recitation or reciting it directly — as among the most effective protective measures available.

The morning and evening adhkar (remembrances) constitute a comprehensive spiritual armor that the Prophet designed for the daily protection of believers. These include: specific recitations of La ilaha ill-Allah, SubhanAllah, Alhamdulillah, Allahu Akbar; the formula of seeking refuge in Allah's perfect words from the evil of His creation (three times); the basmalah and the first three verses of Al-Mu'minun upon waking; and specific protective supplications for entering and leaving the home, eating and drinking, and traveling. Bali notes that the companions who regularly recited these adhkar were, by the Prophet's teaching, under divine protection throughout their days and nights.

Bali also discusses the prophetic teaching on the protective power of the name of Allah (basmalah) specifically in preventing the devil from entering the home, participating in meals, and accompanying travel. The consistent Islamic practice of beginning every significant action with 'Bismillah' (In the name of Allah) is not merely a formality but a conscious invocation of divine protection and blessing that, when done with genuine awareness, genuinely affects the spiritual environment of one's life.

The chapter concludes with the important reminder that protection works through sincere reliance on Allah (tawakkul), not through magical use of Quranic words. The person who recites these protections as ritual formulas without genuine faith and awareness receives far less benefit than the one who recites them with full heart-presence and genuine dependence on Allah.
content_id:
Sarana utama perlindungan terhadap sihir, kerasukan jin, dan ain (mata jahat) dalam Islam adalah bacaan rutin dan penerapan ayat-ayat Al-Quran tertentu serta doa-doa nabawi. Wahid Abdussalam Bali menyajikan resep perlindungan ini bukan sebagai folklor atau takhayul, melainkan sebagai bagian dari Sunnah nabawi — rekomendasi khusus yang diberikan oleh Nabi ﷺ untuk perlindungan orang-orang beriman dari gangguan spiritual.

Ayat Kursi (Al-Baqarah 2:255) menempati posisi tertinggi di antara ayat-ayat perlindungan dalam Al-Quran. Nabi ﷺ menyebutnya sebagai ayat terbesar dalam Al-Quran dan memberikannya makna perlindungan yang luar biasa. Beliau bersabda: 'Barangsiapa membaca Ayat Kursi setelah setiap shalat wajib, tidak ada yang menghalanginya masuk surga kecuali kematian.' Beliau juga mengajarkan bahwa barangsiapa membacanya sebelum tidur akan dilindungi oleh malaikat Allah sepanjang malam. Kandungan teologis ayat ini — penegasannya tentang kedaulatan mutlak Allah, pengetahuan-Nya yang mencakup segala sesuatu, Kursi-Nya yang meliputi langit dan bumi — menjadikannya ungkapan paling kuat dari fondasi-fondasi yang menjadi dasar perlindungan Islam.

Dua surah terakhir dalam Al-Quran — Al-Falaq dan An-Nas, yang secara kolektif disebut al-Muawwidhatayn (dua surah pencari perlindungan) — diberikan oleh Nabi ﷺ aplikasi khusus sebagai bacaan pelindung. Beliau mengajarkan bahwa membacanya tiga kali pagi dan sore memberikan perlindungan sepanjang siang dan malam. Kandungannya secara langsung menangani sumber-sumber bahaya yang dicari perlindungannya oleh orang beriman: Al-Falaq memohon perlindungan dari kejahatan dalam segala sesuatu yang diciptakan, dari kegelapan, dari sihir (khususnya 'kejahatan mereka yang meniup tali-temali'), dan dari ain; An-Nas memohon perlindungan dari bisikan setan, yang membisiki ke dalam hati manusia. Nabi ﷺ dilaporkan membaca surah-surah ini atas dirinya sendiri ketika sakit dan juga merekomendasikannya kepada para sahabatnya.

Surah al-Baqarah secara keseluruhan, atau pembacaannya di rumah, memberikan perlindungan yang kuat menurut beberapa hadis sahih. Nabi ﷺ bersabda: 'Bacalah surah al-Baqarah, karena membacanya adalah berkah dan meninggalkannya adalah penyesalan, dan para tukang sihir tidak dapat mengatasinya.' Beliau juga bersabda: 'Sesungguhnya setan lari dari rumah mana pun yang di dalamnya dibacakan surah al-Baqarah.' Bali merekomendasikan pembacaan al-Baqarah secara rutin di rumah — baik dengan mendengarkan bacaan yang direkam maupun membacanya secara langsung — sebagai salah satu tindakan perlindungan yang paling efektif yang tersedia.

Dzikir pagi dan sore merupakan perisai spiritual komprehensif yang dirancang oleh Nabi ﷺ untuk perlindungan harian orang-orang beriman. Ini mencakup: bacaan-bacaan khusus La ilaha ill-Allah, SubhanAllah, Alhamdulillah, Allahu Akbar; formula memohon perlindungan dalam kata-kata sempurna Allah dari kejahatan ciptaan-Nya (tiga kali); basmalah dan tiga ayat pertama surah Al-Mukminun saat bangun tidur; serta doa-doa pelindung khusus untuk masuk dan keluar rumah, makan dan minum, dan bepergian. Bali mencatat bahwa para sahabat yang secara rutin membaca dzikir ini, menurut ajaran Nabi ﷺ, berada di bawah perlindungan ilahi sepanjang siang dan malam mereka.

Bali juga membahas ajaran nabawi tentang kekuatan perlindungan nama Allah (basmalah) khususnya dalam mencegah setan masuk ke rumah, berpartisipasi dalam makan, dan menyertai perjalanan. Praktik Islam yang konsisten untuk memulai setiap tindakan penting dengan 'Bismillah' (Dengan nama Allah) bukan sekadar formalitas, melainkan invokasi sadar atas perlindungan dan berkah ilahi yang, ketika dilakukan dengan kesadaran yang tulus, benar-benar mempengaruhi lingkungan spiritual kehidupan seseorang.

Bab ini ditutup dengan pengingat penting bahwa perlindungan bekerja melalui kepasrahan yang tulus kepada Allah (tawakkal), bukan melalui penggunaan kata-kata Al-Quran secara magis. Orang yang membaca perlindungan-perlindungan ini sebagai formula ritual tanpa iman dan kesadaran yang tulus menerima manfaat yang jauh lebih sedikit daripada orang yang membacanya dengan kehadiran hati yang penuh dan ketergantungan yang tulus kepada Allah.
title_ar: الحماية من خلال تلاوة القرآن الكريم
content_ar: The الأساسي means of protection against magic, الجن possession, and the الشر eye in الإسلام is the regular التلاوة and application of specific القرآني passages and prophetic supplications. Wahid Abdussalam Bali presents these protective prescriptions not as folklore or superstition but as part of the prophetic السنة — specific recommendations given by النبي ﷺ, ﷺ, for the protection of المؤمنون against spiritual harms. Ayat al-Kursi (Al-Baqarah 2:255) holds the highest position among the protective الآيات of the القرآن الكريم. النبي ﷺ described it as the greatest الآية in the القرآن الكريم and gave it extraordinary protective significance. أفاد: 'Whoever recites Ayat al-Kursi بعد every obligatory الصلاة, nothing will prevent him from entering الجنة except الموت.' He أيضاً taught that whoever recites it قبل sleeping will be protected by an angel of الله throughout the night. The theological content of this الآية — its affirmation of الله's absolute sovereignty, His العلم encompassing all things, His Kursi extending over the heavens and earth — makes it the most powerful expression of the foundations upon which Islamic protection rests. The last two الأبواب of the القرآن الكريم — Al-Falaq and An-Nas, collectively called al-Mu'awwidhatayn (the two الأبواب of seeking refuge) — were given by النبي ﷺ specific applications as protective recitations. He taught that reciting them three times morning and evening provides protection throughout the day and night. Their content directly addresses the sources of harm that the المؤمن seeks protection from: Al-Falaq seeks refuge from the الشر in everything that was created, from darkness, from magic (specifically the 'الشر of those who blow on knots'), and from the الشر eye; An-Nas seeks refuge from the whispering of the الشيطان, who whispers into the القلوب of people. النبي ﷺ reportedly recited these الأبواب over himself عندما ill and أيضاً recommended them to his الصحابة. Surat al-Baqarah as a whole, or its التلاوة in the home, provides powerful protection وفقاً لـ several authentic hadiths. النبي ﷺ stated: 'Recite Surat al-Baqarah, for holding to it is a البركة and abandoning it is a regret, and magicians cannot overcome it.' He أيضاً said: 'Indeed, the الشيطان flees from any house in which Surat al-Baqarah is recited.' Bali recommends regular التلاوة of al-Baqarah in the home — whether by listening to recorded التلاوة or reciting it directly — as among the most effective protective measures available. The morning and evening adhkar (remembrances) constitute a comprehensive spiritual armor that النبي ﷺ designed for the daily protection of المؤمنون. These include: specific recitations of La ilaha ill-الله, سبحان الله, الحمد لله, الله أكبر; the formula of seeking refuge in الله's perfect words from the الشر of His creation (three times); the basmalah and the أولاً three الآيات of Al-Mu'minun upon waking; and specific p
